title: Container fit rules

DCSA_container_types.name: DCSA Shipping Container Types: A Guide
DCSA_container_types.use: Container taxonomy and general purpose by family: dry, reefer, tank, flat rack, open top, etc.

CMA_CGM_container_brochure.name: CMA CGM Container Technical Brochures
CMA_CGM_container_brochure.use: Carrier-specific reference dimensions, gross weight, tare, payload, and container family specs.

ONE_container_specs.name: ONE Container Specifications
ONE_container_specs.use: Cross-check source for dry, reefer, open-top and flat-rack dimensions/payloads.

Hapag_container_offerings.name: Hapag-Lloyd Container Offerings
Hapag_container_offerings.use: Cross-check source for ISO-compliant carrier equipment and special container families.

Maersk_fcl_lcl.name: Maersk FCL vs LCL Shipping Guidance
Maersk_fcl_lcl.use: Planning heuristic for FCL around 15+ CBM and cargo sensitivity/speed factors.

Maersk_lcl_limits.name: Maersk LCL Service Limits
Maersk_lcl_limits.use: Example LCL limits: single package and booking limits; used only as carrier-specific planning reference.

known_limitations: Exact payload and internal dimensions vary by carrier, manufacturer, equipment age and specific container number.
Reference payload must not replace the container CSC plate or carrier booking confirmation.
Flat-rack/open-top dimensional fit does not mean carrier OOG approval is granted.
Reefer suitability requires setpoint, commodity requirements and terminal reefer plug validation.
Tank cargo requires commodity compatibility, cleaning, lining, DG and carrier approval checks.
Live equipment availability, rates and cutoffs are out of scope.
confidence_policy: Use verified_carrier_reference for dimensions/payload from carrier pages; use authored for Cortex logic; cap confidence when exact unit, special approval, reefer plugs or tank compatibility are missing.
